judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are comprehensive and hit all four expectations; they are comparable, with Report 1 slightly cleaner on removing the legacy secret and Report 2 adding extra pipeline gaps.

report_1.evidence: Line 2 — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`. ... Line 4 — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`. ... Line 6 — `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ...` **Remove this key entirely** ... Line 7 — `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ...` **Remove the key.**

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`. ... Remove it (set to empty). ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ...` Change to a prod sink (`.../prod/...`) or empty it

report_1.evidence: the production frontend ships with `https://staging-api.example.com` compiled into it. Real production users' browsers would call the staging API. Because the URL is baked at build time, there is no runtime fix — the frontend must be **rebuilt** for production

report_2.evidence: So the API base URL in the shipped production bundle is `https://staging-api.example.com` ... deploy-prod deploys **the same digest** ... Fix: add a `frontend/.env.production` with the prod API URL and build the prod artifact with `--mode production`

report_1.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` says "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs migrations ... don't assume "pipeline succeeded" means the schema is current.

report_2.evidence: The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md:6`) says "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the workflow has no migration step ... On a fresh prod DB nothing meaningful gets created.

`infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied unchanged and still sets `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` ... Fix: set prod's `oidc_sub_condition` to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`
